https://www.kcci.com/article/71-year-old-iowan-reaches-goal-of-1-million-ab-exercises-in-2020/35068756

I can only give you my perspective, but here it is:  I tried to mix things up.  Try to keep you off balance.  Maybe start you with a change out of the zone followed by a drop ball on the outside corner.  Then a rise ball up and in - not a strike.  I tried NEVER to throw any pitch in the "happy zone" or across the plate.  I knew I had to mix things up because you could hit the ball out of the park to any field; couldn't get in a pattern with you ever.  If I'm remembering correctly, the HR you hit at Birdland in 1976 was a rise ball on the outer half of the plate.  It wasn't a bad pitch; you simply hit it HARD!  But I saw you hit lots of those, 39.  The faster the pitcher, the better you liked them.
 
But if I could get my change mixed in properly with my other pitches, I had a chance to get you out.  Like I said, I wanted to try you keep you off balance.  The challenge for me was how quickly you swung the bat; you had great bat speed and very quick reaction time. Bottom line: great hitter and you and I had some great battles.
 
Does that help, Peter?
